concepts.title: Data Independence
concepts.description: Data independence refers to the ability of the database to remain unchanged even if the physical storage or logical structure of the database is modified.

concepts.title: Database Types
concepts.description: A database can be classified into three types: flat file database, hierarchical database, and relational database.

concepts.title: Normalization
concepts.description: Normalization is the process of organizing the data in a database to minimize data redundancy and dependency.

concepts.title: Codd's Rules
concepts.description: To remember Codd's 12 rules for relational databases, use the sentence: 'Every rule is a virtue except one' and associate each word with a rule.

concepts.title: Data Normalization Error
concepts.description: Students often make the mistake of normalizing data to the point where the database becomes overly complex and difficult to understand. To avoid this, ensure that the data is normalized only to the point where it is necessary.

concepts.title: Relational Algebra Operations
concepts.description: Relational algebra operations include: selection (σ), projection (π), union (∪), intersection (∩), difference (-), Cartesian product (×), and division (÷).

concepts.title: Database Security
concepts.description: Database security involves controlling access to the database by using techniques such as authentication, authorization, and encryption.

concepts.title: SQL Queries
concepts.description: SQL queries can be classified into two types: DDL (Data Definition Language) and DML (Data Manipulation Language) queries.
